result.harnessNotes: This wrapp is bespoke (not the take.js template). Autostart is CONDITIONAL on a lent brand: on connect (chip onConnect OR the returning-user probe when permissions() returns a grant) bootConnected() runs storage-restore → loadBrandCtx() → maybeAutoDraft(). maybeAutoDraft fires the brand-draft stream with ZERO clicks ONLY if relay is connected AND a brand context resolved AND the wall is empty (or the stored brandName differs from the lent brand name). To drive stage 1 headlessly the mock MUST: (1) permissions()/relay.permissions() resolve truthy (a grant) so the probe boots; (2) context.active() resolve a kind:\"brand\" context, e.g. {name:\"Allbirds\", kind:\"brand\", data:{name, voice|vibe, positioning, audience, palette:[\"#...\",...] (FLAT hex strings) or paletteRich:[{hex}], products|range:[...]}} — if active() returns null it falls back to context.list() (find first meta whose (kind||'').toLowerCase()===\"brand\") then context.use(id); with no brand at all there is NO autostart and you'd have to click #draft-url (URL path, needs WebFetch). (3) storage.get(\"adgen:state\") should resolve null (fresh) so nothing pre-populates the wall (a non-empty stored directions array with the same brandName suppresses autostart). relay.stream(params) is an async iterator; params for stage 1 is {prompt, agentic:false}. Yield deltas of shape {type:\"text\",text} whose concatenation contains ONLY a JSON object {\"directions\":[ ... ]} — parseWall strips ```fences, regex-grabs the outer {…}, JSON.parses, and REQUIRES the directions array length >= 3 (else \"came back malformed\"); it slices to 6, coerces each item's name/angle/headline(≤48 chars)/imagePrompt/format(must be one of \"1:1\"|\"9:16\"|\"16:9\", else defaults 1:1)/recommended(boolean), and forces exactly one recommended:true. Delta types the loop also handles: {type:\"tool_proposed\",call:{name}}, {type:\"tool_result\",call:{name},result:{ok,content:[{text}],error:{message}}}, {type:\"error\",error:{message}}, {type:\"done\"}. URL-draft stage is agentic:true and its JSON object additionally needs a top-level \"brand\":{name,product,tone,colors:[hex]} sibling to directions; it may emit a WebFetch tool_result whose result.ok + content[].text gets banked. Render stage is agentic:true; extract succeeds from EITHER a tool_result (result.ok, content[].text containing an image URL) OR the final text — URL must match /https?:...\\.(png|jpe?g|webp)/ or a JSON field \"url\"|\"rawUrl\"|\"minUrl\":\"...\". Scope requested: tools [\"WebFetch\",\"mcp__claude_ai_Higgsfield__*\"], contextKinds [\"brand\"], no models.

result.harnessNotes:
CUSTOM PLUMBING — this wrapp does NOT use take.js's relay.storage/askJson helpers; it talks to relay.stream directly and persists to plain browser localStorage (STORE_KEY 'adpulse:v1', PREFIX_KEY 'adpulse:meta-prefix'). Mock window.claude does NOT need a storage API.

REQUIRED relay surface: connect(scope)->resolves a grant; permissions()->truthy grant object (ideally {tools:[{name:'mcp__..._*'}]} so grantCoversCachedPrefix can match); context.active()->ctx|null; context.list()->[{id,kind,name}] (code does .find(m=>m.kind.toLowerCase()==='brand')); context.use(id)->ctx; context.pick()->ctx (only on #brand-switch click); stream(params)-> async iterator of deltas {type:'text',text}|{type:'tool_proposed',call:{name}}|{type:'tool_result',result:{ok,content?,error?}}|{type:'error',error:{message}}. Connect scope on mount is {reason,models:['sonnet'],contextKinds:['brand']}; the live-pull re-connect is {reason,tools:[prefix+'*'],models:['sonnet']}.

BOOT: on load it ALWAYS ingests the embedded SAMPLE (12 Verra Skincare INR campaigns) unless localStorage has a saved csv, so the instant tape (#tape/#stats) is visible with zero AI and zero connect. Returning-user probe = whenRelayReady(2000) then permissions(); if a grant returns it sets relay, loads brand, and calls proactiveKickoff(false).

AUTOSTART NUANCE (why bootsHeadless=false): with the default sample loaded and NO cached PREFIX_KEY, proactiveKickoff(false) fires NO model call — the wrapp just enables #analyse and waits. A model call only autostarts when: (a) a REAL user gesture clicks the connect chip (sets chipGesture=true) -> proactiveKickoff(true) -> pullLive() -> DISCOVER stage runs first; or (b) restored REAL (non-sample) data exists -> analyse() runs; or (c) cached prefix + grant covers it -> pullLive().

SIMPLEST HEADLESS DRIVE TO A VISIBLE RESULT: grant present + sample already loaded => #analyse is enabled => invoke analyse() (click #analyse). This fires exactly ONE stream call = the DIAGNOSIS stage (NON-agentic, no tools). Mock returns one JSON object (extracted via /\{[\s\S]*\}/ then JSON.parse) with shape {score:0-100 int, headline:str, wins:[{title,detail}], leaks:[{title,detail,monthlyBurn:number}], actions:[{title,impact:'high'|'medium',effort:'low'|'medium'|'high',detail}], campaigns:[{name,verdict:'scale'|'keep'|'fix'|'kill',note}]}. normalize() tolerates missing/invalid fields (verdict defaults 'keep', impact/effort default 'medium'); campaigns[].name should echo names from the CSV for a natural verdict table. This skips discover+pull entirely.

FULL LIVE PATH (3 model interactions): (1) DISCOVER — stream({agentic:true}) prompt asking for the connector prefix; mock yields {type:'text',text:'mcp__claude_ai_Meta_Ads__'} (must match /mcp__[A-Za-z0-9_]+__(?!_)/, or 'NONE' to fail). Result is cached in PREFIX_KEY so this stage is skipped on subsequent pulls. (2) relay.connect({tools:[prefix+'*'],...}) must resolve. (3) PULL — stream({agentic:true}) with PULL_PROMPT; mock yields {type:'text'} accumulating a CSV whose first non-blank line matches /^\s*\"?Campaign name\"?\s*,/ and has >=2 lines (optionally emit tool_proposed/tool_result deltas first — tool_result.ok drives a status line). Header currency code in 'Amount spent (XXX)' sets displayed currency (INR fallback). To signal failure, return a line starting 'PULL-FAILED: <reason>'. After a successful pull it auto-ingests and auto-calls analyse(), so the pull path also needs the diagnosis JSON to reach #report.

No images/video. No agentic tool_result content needed for the diagnosis stage (it is a plain non-agentic text stream).

The #statusbox (which showed status-line \"Sketching three directions…\") hides when the run resolves.
result.harnessNotes: AUTOSTARTS: no one-line input needed. The returning-user probe `whenRelayReady(2000)` calls `r.permissions()`; if it returns any truthy grant, the mock boots into onRelay() which calls maybeAutoRun() → generateDirections() (stage 1) unprompted. So window.claude MUST return a non-null grant from permissions() to boot headless (fresh chip-connect onConnect also triggers it). Stage 1 fires even with NO brand: onRelay sets #f-line to SAMPLE_LINE and currentProduct() returns it, so a bare connect still autoruns. To exercise zero-typing brand mode, have relay.context.active() resolve a ctx whose data.products[0] becomes the product; else return null (falls back to SAMPLE_LINE). relay.context.list() is called with autoSelect and filtered by m.kind===\"brand\" (tolerates throw). STREAM CONTRACT: the wrapp calls `relay.stream({ prompt, agentic:true })` and expects an async iterator yielding deltas `{type:'text', text}` (accumulated), optional `{type:'tool_proposed', call:{name}}`, `{type:'tool_result', result:{ok, error}}`, `{type:'error', error:{message, code}}`. It does NOT read a 'done' delta — just end iteration. The accumulated text is code-fence-stripped then matched with /\\{[\\s\\S]*\\}/ and JSON.parse'd, so the response is ALWAYS a single JSON OBJECT (never a bare array), and may be wrapped in prose/fences. Stage 1 object MUST have `directions`: an array of >=2 (ideally 3) of {name, heroHeadline, angle, chartArgues, recommended:true|false} with exactly one recommended. Stage 2 (generateStack) is NOT autostarted — it only fires when a direction card is clicked (pickDirection→generateStack); its object needs heroHeadline, heroSub, features[4]{emoji,title,body}, comparison{ourName,otherName,rows[5]{feature,ours,other}}, brandStory{headline,body}, faqs[3]{q,a}, searchTerms[8-12]; missing any top-level key throws INCOMPLETE. Stage 3 module-regens fire only on the rg-* buttons. No images/video. WebFetch tool is only invoked if the single input line is a URL (lineUrl() detects it) — not needed to drive the default flow. Persistence uses relay.storage.get/set with key \"aplus:v2\"; a NEWER savedAt in relay.storage re-hydrates over the local mirror. Scope requested: models:['sonnet'], tools:['WebFetch'], contextKinds:['brand'].

result.successSignal: The #reading section un-hides (document.getElementById('reading').hidden === false) with #opening showing the model's opening line, each .take element inside #slot-0/1/2 filled with a card interpretation, and #synthesis/#advice/#omen populated; the #read button label flips to \"Re-read the spread\".
result.harnessNotes: Single-stage, autostart wrapp — no one-line input needed. Mock window.claude must expose: connect (SDK checks \"connect\" in r), permissions() resolving a TRUTHY grant (this is what triggers the returning-user headless boot at src/arcana.js:307-322 → autoRun), identity() → optional {name}, context.list() → array (may be [] — filtered by kind \"personal\"/\"project\"), context.active() → null or a personal-kind context, context.use(id) → context with .data, storage.get(\"arcana:reading:v1\") and storage.set(k,v). CRITICAL: storage.get MUST resolve null/empty — if it returns a JSON string with a newer t plus a valid spread+reading, autoRun restores it and the `if (reading) return` guard skips the model call entirely, so stage 1 never fires. The spread is drawn CLIENT-SIDE with crypto.getRandomValues before the call; the mock does not parse the drawn cards, it just returns a reading. The ONE model call is relay.stream({prompt}) — an async iterator yielding {type:\"text\", text} deltas (and optionally {type:\"error\", error:{message,code}}). Concatenated text must contain a JSON OBJECT (extracted via /\\{[\\s\\S]*\\}/) shaped {\"opening\":<non-empty string>,\"cards\":[{\"position\":\"past\",\"take\":\"…\"},{\"position\":\"present\",\"take\":\"…\"},{\"position\":\"future\",\"take\":\"…\"}],\"synthesis\":\"…\",\"advice\":\"…\",\"omen\":\"…\"}. Validation throws a visible \"malformed\" error unless opening is truthy AND cards is an array with length>=3; card takes are matched to slots by position containing past/present/future (else by index). No agentic/tools, no images, no video. Providing identity name / a personal context with data.fullName/company/notes or project metas only personalizes the prompt and question chips — omitting them keeps querent=null and still drives a full anonymous reading.

result.successSignal:
#brief-out becomes visible (hidden=false) containing one or more
bullets, and #brief-note text reads exactly \"today — from your notes + open tasks\". If the recommended string matches an open task, a ★ do this first also appears (in #brief-out and on the matching board .trow). #brief-sec is unhidden.
result.harnessNotes:
This wrapp does NOT wait for input to make its first call — it autostarts. To drive it headlessly the mock window.claude must:

BOOT: permissions() must resolve to a truthy grant object (the returning-user probe at file bottom auto-calls enterVault() on a grant). on(event,cb) must exist (subscribes to \"permissionsChanged\"; wrap-safe if it throws). connect() and identity() must exist. identity() should resolve {name:\"...\"} (only used to seed starter-note labels).

VAULT READ (Promise.all in doBoot): 
- context.list() → return [] to keep ONLY the brief stream firing on autostart. (If you return any meta with kind:\"project\", a SECOND non-agentic stream — the auto daily pull, stage 4 shape — also fires via maybeAutoSync unless storage.get(\"sync-stamp\") === today's yyyy-mm-dd. Return [] to avoid it.)
- storage.list() → MUST return at least one key ending in \".md\" (e.g. [\"n-plan.md\"]). This is the load-bearing read; if it rejects, the UI shows an error+retry state instead of the vault. Include \"tasks.md\" too if you want board tasks.
- storage.info() → return {folder:\"~/Vault\", autoAssigned:false} (autoAssigned:true just shows a bind prompt; harmless).
- storage.get(key) → for each .md key return markdown text with a non-empty body (parseNote drops notes whose body.trim() is empty). Put a \"- [ ] some task\" line in it so recommended-matching can light the ★. 
- storage.get(\"brief.json\") → MUST return null (or a JSON string whose .date != today) or the brief is served from cache and NO model call happens.
- storage.get(\"ask-last.json\") → null. storage.get(\"sync-stamp\") → null (or today to suppress auto-sync).
- storage.set / storage.delete / storage.bind → resolve ok (set is called to persist brief.json after success; non-fatal if it throws).

STAGE 1 STREAM (the autostart call): relay.stream({prompt}) with NO agentic flag. It is an async iterator; yield {type:\"text\", text: '{\"brief\":[\"bullet one\",\"bullet two\"],\"recommended\":\"some task\"}'} then stop (a {type:\"done\"} is fine/optional). Code strips ``` fences, regex-matches the first {...}, JSON.parses it, requires Array.isArray(v.brief) && v.brief.length>0 (else it renders a 'brief didn't land' error+retry). recommended is normalized (lowercased, strips ' — by <due>' suffix) and matched against open-task text to stamp the ★. Route this canned response by the words \"today's brief\"/\"3-5 short\"/\"concrete bullets\" (the sync stage also says \"chief of staff\", so don't route on that alone).

OTHER STAGES (only if you exercise them):
- Ask: manual (#ask-in + #ask-go / Enter). Plain-text stream; whatever text you yield is shown verbatim in #ask-out. Not JSON.
- discoverChannels: fired by #sync-go button, agentic:true. Return {type:\"text\"} with a JSON object mapping gmail/whatsapp/granola → an mcp tool-prefix string matching /^mcp__[A-Za-z0-9_]+__$/ (e.g. \"mcp__abcd1234__\") or null. Cached in localStorage 24h under \"bank:channel-prefixes\".
- sync pull: agentic:true, buildSyncPrompt. Return {type:\"text\"} with a JSON ARRAY of {\"text\":\"...\",\"source\":\"Gmail|WhatsApp|Granola|<project>\",\"due\":\"...\"}. A denied tool surfaces as {type:\"tool_result\", result:{ok:false, error:{message}}}; a proposed call as {type:\"tool_proposed\", call:{name}}. If found channels exist it first calls relay.connect({tools:[...]}) which must resolve.

No images/video. Stream deltas use the shape {type:\"text\"|\"tool_proposed\"|\"tool_result\"|\"error\"|\"done\"}; error is {type:\"error\", error:{message}}.

result.name: Batch
result.successSignal: The hero hides and #view shows a .runbar with kicker \"the idea\" + the brief. The first .q-card (label span .q-num \"question 1\", .q-text with the 50-char question) renders a .opts block of three .opt cards, each with .o-label and .o-text; exactly one card shows a .rec badge reading \"recommended\". For Q1 specifically each .o-label also gets a .charcount span reading \"N chars\" (with class \"over\" when >50). While drafting, a .researching row shows status like \"drafting 1 of 8 — …\".
result.harnessNotes:
AUTOSTART GATE: on connect, onReady()→autostart() fires the first model call ONLY if a lent context is active. So the mock window.claude MUST make relay.context.active() resolve to an object like {name:"Acme", data:{positioning:"…"}}; autostart() then seeds brief = name + " — " + data.positioning and calls start() with ZERO user input, drafting all 8 questions sequentially (draftOne 1→8), then the 2 video scripts. If context.active() returns null, nothing auto-runs — the start screen waits for a one-line input + "Draft all 8 ▸" button before any model call. bootsHeadless is true via the returning-user probe (whenRelayReady→r.permissions() returning a grant calls onReady without a chip click), but a lent context is required for a fully headless first stage.

STREAM SHAPE: every model call goes through relay.stream({prompt}) as an async iterator; the wrapp only reads deltas of {type:"text",text} and accumulates, then parseJsonArray() slices from first '[' to last ']'. So yield one or more {type:"text",text:"<json array string>"} deltas then end the iterator (a {type:"done"} is fine/ignored; {type:"error",error:{message}} throws). NOT relay.complete — it uses stream only. Response must be a JSON ARRAY of exactly the objects {"label":string(2-5 words),"text":string,"recommended":boolean} — code takes arr.slice(0,3), forces one recommended if none, and pre-selects the recommended one. Fences (```json) are stripped, so they're tolerated.

ROUTING: the same canned 3-element array satisfies all 8 draft_answer calls AND both draft_video_script calls; distinguish stage 1 (answers) by "complete answer options"/"drafting a y combinator application" vs the video stage by "prepping the videos"/"script options". Per-question routing is optional (all 8 read label/text/recommended identically); Q1's guide asks for a ≤50-char one-liner in text but any string renders.

STORAGE: relay.storage.get("batch-state") should return null on first run (else a saved run restores and autostart just clears status, drawing cards from storage with NO model call). relay.storage.set is called after each landing — a no-op stub is fine.

NO IMAGES/VIDEO GEN: genImage()/agentic Higgsfield path exists but is never called (scope.tools=[]). The two YC "videos" are recorded locally via kit/recorder.js (getDisplayMedia/getUserMedia) and only mount AFTER the 8 answers + 2 scripts land — not needed to reach the stage-1 visible result and safe to leave unstubbed in a headless run.

result.name: Cartridge

stages.name: remix (change an existing game)
promptKeywords: here is the current complete game

result.successSignal: #pitches un-hides and #pitch-grid fills with four .pitch cards (each .pt title + .pi idea); exactly one is .pitch.star and shows text \"★ RECOMMENDED\". That is the autostart (pitch-completion) result. On a game forge/GO, #cabinet un-hides, #g-title shows the uppercased title, and the #stage iframe srcdoc holds the generated HTML.
result.harnessNotes:
FIRST CALL ON AUTOSTART IS relay.complete(), NOT relay.stream(). Boot path: a returning-user probe runs whenRelayReady(2000) then r.permissions() — the mock MUST return a truthy grant from permissions() so the app boots headless with zero clicks (it then calls reflectConn + onRelayLive). onRelayLive → restoreFromStorage() → ensurePitches().

STAGE 1 (autostart, the pitch deck): ensurePitches() only skips the model call if relay.storage.get(\"cartridge:pitches\") returns an envelope {at, pitches:[...]} younger than 24h — so to force the live call, storage.get must return null/empty for ALL keys (cartridge:cart, cartridge:shelf, cartridge:form, cartridge:pitches). Before the completion, rollPitches() calls relay.context.active() (may return null), and if null relay.context.list() (return [] to skip personalization; if you return metas, one whose .kind matches /personal|brand|project/ triggers relay.context.use(id) and its .name/.kind/.data get stitched into the prompt with 'starring' asks). Then it calls relay.complete({ prompt, system, maxTokens:900, effort:'low', model:'sonnet' }) and reads res.text ONLY. res.text must be a STRING containing a JSON array of exactly 4 objects; parsePitches reads keys title, idea, twist, genre, vibe, recommended(bool), starring(bool). genre must be one of [platformer,shooter,puzzle,arcade,racer,snake-like,breakout,dodge-em-up] and vibe one of [neon,retro pixel,mono CRT,pastel,vaporwave] or it silently coerces to arcade/neon. Markdown fences are stripped. IMPORTANT resilience note: if res.text is unparseable the app falls back to dicePitches() and STILL renders 4 cards — so the pitch grid appears even on a bad mock, but return valid JSON to exercise the real path.

STAGE 2 (game gen — only after a click on #go, a pitch card's '▶ FORGE THIS' button, or programmatic): relay.stream({ prompt, system, maxTokens:16000, effort:'low', model:'sonnet' }) is an async iterator; the mock must yield deltas {type:'text', text:'...'} whose CONCATENATION contains a full HTML document — extractHtml requires a substring matching /<!doctype html|<html[\\s>]/i through the last </html>. Optionally include <title>NAME</title> (1-60 chars) — titleOf reads it for the cartridge title. Yield {type:'error', error:{message,code}} to test error UI. On success #stage.srcdoc = html and #cabinet un-hides.

STAGE 3 (remix): same relay.stream shape; triggered by typing into #remix-in and clicking #remix. Prompt embeds the prior game HTML in ```html fences and asks for the full updated document.

MODEL FALLBACK: every call passes model:'sonnet'; if the mock throws an error whose Number(err.code) === BYOPErrorCode.SCOPE_EXCEEDED, the app retries the SAME call with model omitted (null). A simple mock can just ignore the model param.

REQUIRED window.claude / relay surface: connect, permissions(), stream() (async iterator), complete() (resolves {text}), storage.get(key)->string|null and storage.set(key,val), context.active()/list()/use(id), and r.on(...) is only used in the take template — cartridge.js does NOT call relay.on, so it's optional here. No images, no video, no agentic tool_result needed — the generated game runs in a sandboxed iframe and never touches window.claude.

result.name: betterchat
result.successSignal: After stage 1, the #chips container (currently holding disabled 'sample' chips) is replaced with clickable .chip buttons of real starter text — exactly one has class 'rec' and a '★ ' prefix — followed by a '↻ more like these' button; #suggest-note changes from 'sample — connect to make these yours' to either 'from “<ctxName>”' or 'generic — lend a context for sharper starters'. Concurrently #greet-hi reads 'Hi <name>' and the #prompt composer is enabled.
result.harnessNotes: AUTOSTART: on chip onConnect OR on page-load probe when relay.permissions() resolves a truthy grant, onReady() runs and fires the FIRST model call (starter_suggestions) with ZERO user input. To boot headless the mock must implement relay.permissions() returning a non-null grant (probe path calls whenRelayReady(2000) then r.permissions()). Also required on the relay object: identity() -> {name} (greeting; wrapped in .catch so optional); storage.get(key)/storage.set(key,val) with string values (key 'betterchat:v1', JSON in/out; return null/empty for a fresh user); context.active() -> the lent context or null; context.list() -> array of metas {id,kind,updatedAt} used only if active() is null (bestMeta ranks by KIND_PRIORITY then updatedAt, any kind is selectable); context.use(id) -> full context {id,name,kind,data}. The wrapp reads ctx.data via JSON.stringify(ctx.data) wholesale (capped 4000 chars) — data can be any object; no specific data.* subkeys are accessed. ROUTING: the two stages are distinguished purely by relay.stream() params — starter_suggestions passes {prompt:<string>, maxTokens:600} (route on params.prompt present); chat_turn passes {messages:[{role,content}...], system:<string>, maxTokens:4000} and NO prompt (route on params.messages present, match keywords inside params.system). STREAM SHAPE: relay.stream(params) must be an async iterator yielding deltas {type:'text', text:'...'} and optionally end/{type:'error',error:{message}}; there is NO relay.complete() call and NO agentic/tool_result/image path. STAGE 1 RESPONSE: concatenated text deltas must form a JSON array (code strips ``` fences and slices first '[' to last ']'); each element either a bare string OR {text:string, recommended:boolean}; code keeps up to 5, truncates text to 120 chars, and forces exactly one recommended (defaults first). If parse yields nothing the wrapp silently falls back to built-in fallbackSuggestions() and still renders chips — so a malformed stage-1 response still 'succeeds' visually; to prove the MOCK drove it, return distinctive text not in the SAMPLES/fallback lists. STAGE 2 RESPONSE: plain streamed markdown text; rendered live into an assistant bubble (.msg.assistant .m-body) via mdLite and persisted to storage. Stage 2 only fires after the user sends a message or clicks a starter chip.

Reply is plain prose — NOT JSON; it is rendered verbatim as textContent, so return conversational text. (2) The ask() stream params carry sessionId (a warm 'huddle-<uid>' session reused across turns), system (starts 'You are on a live working call with the founder…'), prompt (= the user's typed line), maxTokens:1200 — mock can ignore sessionId and just answer prompt. HOWEVER draftConcepts() early-returns unless a brand context exists (`if (!relay || drafting || !brand) return`), and maybeAutoDraft() with no brand only prefills a STARTER prompt and never calls the model. So to drive stage 1 headlessly the mock window.claude MUST supply a brand context via ONE of: relay.context.active() resolving to {id, name, data:{...}}, OR relay.context.list() returning at least one meta whose kind (case-insensitive) === "brand" PLUS relay.context.use(id) resolving to the full context. normalizeBrand reads ctx.name, ctx.id and ctx.data.{products|range, styles, name, voice|vibe|positioning, palette} — palette must be an array of flat hex strings. STAGE 1 STREAM CONTRACT: relay.stream({prompt, agentic:true}) must be an async iterator yielding deltas {type:'text', text:'...'} (optionally {type:'error',error:{message}}). The accumulated text must contain a JSON OBJECT (not a bare array) shaped {"concepts":[ ...at least 3, ideally 6 items... ]}. parseConcepts strips ```fences, regex-matches the first {...} block, then coerceConcept REQUIRES each item to have non-empty string `title` AND non-empty string `imagePrompt` (items missing either are dropped); if fewer than 3 survive it throws 'malformed concepts' and shows the Retry bar. `aspect` must be one of '1:1'|'16:9'|'9:16' (else coerced to '1:1'). relay.storage.get/set must exist and deal in STRING values (JSON stringified); get() may reject/return null on first run (localStorage tier stands). context.active/list/use may each throw and the app falls through gracefully.

Its stream is agentic and the mock should emit {type:'tool_proposed', call:{name:'...generate_image'}} then {type:'tool_result', result:{ok:true, content:[{text:'https://x/y.png'}]}, call:{name}} — the final URL is extracted via regex from resultText (result.content[].text) OR from accumulated {type:'text'} deltas (a bare https URL ending .png/.jpg/.jpeg/.webp, or a JSON "url"/"rawUrl"/"minUrl" field). The connector wildcard is mcp__claude_ai_Higgsfield__*. If a reference image is attached the loop also expects media_upload → put_blob(handle:'ref') → media_confirm tool calls, but the base render needs only generate_image + a URL. Scope requested at connect: tools=['mcp__claude_ai_Higgsfield__*'], contextKinds=['brand'], context 'single'.

result.successSignal: The #mq-frame iframe's srcdoc is populated with the generated landing-page HTML (streamed live as text accumulates). On completion (running=false, r.html set) the runbar shows a \"⬇ download .html\" button and the view appends the kicker text \"refine it — describe any change\".
result.harnessNotes:
Plumbing is the byte-identical /wrapp template (same as take.js): mock window.claude must expose connect/permissions()/on()/context.active()/storage.get|set and stream(). On the returning-user probe the wrapp calls r.permissions(); return a truthy grant so it auto-boots into onReady()→autostart() with no chip click.

AUTOSTART CONDITION: autostart() only launches the first model call (generate) if a lent brand context exists. So context.active() must resolve to an object like {name:\"Acme\", data:{positioning:\"...\", palette:[\"#111\",\"#eee\",\"#f30\"]}}. The seed becomes \"a landing page for <name> — <positioning>\". With NO brand, nothing runs on connect — the harness must type into the \"one line — what's the landing page for?\" input and click \"Build the page ▸\" (or press Enter) to fire start()→generate().

STAGE 1 (generate) uses relay.stream({prompt,maxTokens:8000}) and reads plain streamed text (type:\"text\" deltas), NOT JSON. The canned response must be a raw HTML document string starting with <!doctype html> and MUST (a) satisfy /<[a-z]/i, and (b) contain id=\"hero-img\" with a style attribute so the hero stage can weave in the image. Fences are stripped. The iframe srcdoc is updated mid-stream every ~400 chars, so a single final delta is fine.

STAGE 2 (paintHero) only runs if r.html.includes(\"hero-img\"). It calls relay.stream({prompt,agentic:true}). Return either a delta {type:\"tool_result\", result:{ok:true, content:[{text:\"https://x/hero.png\"}]}} OR a {type:\"text\"} delta whose text contains a URL matching IMG_URL_RE (ends in .png/.jpg/.jpeg/.webp, or a JSON \"rawUrl\"/\"url\"/\"minUrl\":\"...\"). For the URL to actually splice into the page the HTML's hero div must match /(id=[\"']hero-img[\"'][^>]*style=[\"'][^\"']*)/i (i.e. id=\"hero-img\" ... style=\"...\"). This stage is optional polish; failure is swallowed, so it is not required for the success signal.

STAGE 3 (refine) is user-triggered (typing into the \"refine it\" input / Edit button), not autostart. relay.stream returns text that must be a JSON object {\"find\":<exact unique substring present in the first 12000 chars of r.html>,\"replace\":<edited substring>}. Code reads out.find and out.replace; applyEdit requires find to be a unique substring (or whitespace-flexible single match) of the current HTML.

Only relay.stream is used (no relay.complete). Tools scope requests the Higgsfield wildcard mcp__claude_ai_Higgsfield__* and models:[\"sonnet\"]; a headless mock can ignore tool gating and just emit the canned tool_result.

Each stream must yield {type:"text",text} deltas whose ACCUMULATED text contains a JSON OBJECT (the code does acc.match(/\\{[\\s\\S]*\\}/) then JSON.parse — a bare JSON array will NOT match; wrap in {}). result.successSignal: After the auto-audit resolves, #side-body fills with one or more `.dec` cards (each having `.dec-num` with the finding number, `.dec-target`, `.dec-snip`), and #dec-count shows text like "5 open"; a toast reads "Audit ✓ N suggestions pinned — open a card, the fix is ready to lock". Before the audit resolves the sidebar shows the `.empty` block ("No comments yet…"); while running it shows a `.researching` row "auditing the whole page — pinning suggestions…". Pinned elements in the #frame iframe also gain green `[data-redline]` numbered outline badges.
result.harnessNotes:
Redline is NOT the standard single-input template — it is project/folder-bound and reads NO data.* payload. The "context" it consumes is kind "project" metas, and it only uses each meta's `folder` (load-bearing, binds storage) and `name` (card label); it never reads the project's data. So the mock's context lending matters only for the folder.

AUTOSTART REQUIRES A BOUND FOLDER. On a returning-user grant the IIFE probes relay.permissions() (must resolve truthy) → onReady → resolveProject → relay.storage.info(). To reach a MODEL call headlessly the mock MUST return info = { autoAssigned:false, folder:"/path" }. Then relay.storage.list() must return an array containing at least one ".html" key (prefer "index.html" — it is auto-selected), and relay.storage.get("index.html") must return an HTML string. relay.storage.get(reviewKey) where reviewKey = "redline-"+slug(pageKey) (e.g. "redline-index") must return null/"" so lastAuditAt stays 0 and audit() auto-fires. If info is autoAssigned/no-folder instead, no model call happens — it renders bind cards from relay.context.list() (metas filtered to kind==="project" with a folder) and waits for a click (bindFolder → relay.storage.bind(path) must resolve {folder}).

STAGE 1 (audit) is what runs on autostart. It calls relay.stream({prompt, maxTokens:8000}) — a plain async iterator of {type:"text",text} deltas (no agentic/tools). Accumulated text is parsed as a JSON ARRAY (parseJsonArray strips ``` fences and slices [..]). Return 5–10 objects: {tag, snippet (EXACT visible text of an element, appears in the HTML so locateBySnippet can find the node), issue (blunt sentence → becomes the pinned note), label, find, replace, preview}. For a card to arrive pre-seeded/ready-to-lock, `find` must be a verbatim substring of the returned HTML with length ≥ 8 AND currentHtml.includes(find) true, and `replace` non-null. snippet+issue are the minimum required per finding (others optional).

STAGE 2 (respond-router) only fires when the user types a comment and clicks Ask Redline — not headless. relay.stream({prompt}) plain; parsed as a single JSON OBJECT with `mode` ∈ edit|image|references|reply. edit → {mode,summary,find,options:[{label,replace,preview,recommended}]} (find must appear verbatim once in currentHtml). image → {mode,brief}. references → {mode,query}. reply → {mode,markdown}.

AGENTIC stages (references, image-mockup, publish, and lock-time image-placement) call relay.stream({prompt, agentic:true}). Image gen reads {type:"tool_result", result:{ok:true, content:[{text:"…https://….png…"}]}} and pulls the URL via regex /(https?:…\.(png|jpe?g|webp))|"(?:rawUrl|url|minUrl)":"…"/; falls back to accumulated {type:"text"} text. Publish returns text containing JSON {ok:true, sha, changes} (or {ok:false,error}). None of these are on the autostart path; image mockups need HIGGSFIELD tool grant (mcp__claude_ai_Higgsfield__*).

Mock must also implement relay.on("permissionsChanged", cb) (wireRelay subscribes; syncProject re-reads storage.info) and relay.storage.set(key,val) resolving (saveReview + lock writes call it). mountConnect/whenRelayReady come from @relay/sdk. Scope granted: models ["sonnet"], tools [Higgsfield wildcard, WebSearch, WebFetch], contextKinds ["project"].

AUTOSTART GATE — the first model call only fires headlessly if a brand context is lent. onReady()→autostart() calls start() only when `brand` is truthy; the seed is literally "a launch reel for "+brand.name+(brand.data.positioning?" — "+positioning:""). So the mock window.claude must make relay.context.active() resolve to an object {name:"...", data:{positioning:"...", palette:["#hex1","#hex2","#hex3"]}}. With NO lent brand, connect does NOT run any model call — the page just renders a one-line input (placeholder "one line — what's the reel for?"); the pipeline then starts on Enter/"Make the reel ▸" via start(inputValue). bootsHeadless=true refers to the returning-user probe re-connecting (whenRelayReady→permissions()→onReady) without a chip click, but the FIRST model call still requires the lent brand. Storage: loadState reads key "reel-state" (APP.id+"-state"); return null/empty for a fresh run so state.run starts null and autostart proceeds. STAGE 1 (script) — relay.stream({prompt}) NON-agentic. Prompt built by askJsonArray joining the parts with "\n\n"; distinctive words above. Must stream {type:"text",text:"..."} deltas whose concatenation contains a JSON ARRAY (fences tolerated, parser strips ```). Each element read for keys: title (≤6 words → sliced 60), subtitle (sliced 120), imageBrief (sliced 300), seconds (clamped 2–4, default 3). Return 4–6 elements; code takes arr.slice(0,6). result.harnessNotes: CRITICAL: stage 1 is triggered by a BRAND LANDING, not by any text input. The pipeline autostarts headlessly: boot IIFE calls whenRelayReady(2000) then relay.permissions(); if that returns a truthy grant it runs onConnected → syncFromRelayStorage → loadBrand → applyBrand → generateShotList(false). So the mock provider MUST: (1) expose connect, permissions (return a non-null grant object for headless boot), on(evt,cb) (needs \"permissionsChanged\"), storage{get,set,delete}, context, complete, stream. (2) relay.context.active() must resolve a brand context, OR — to exercise the auto-select path — active() returns null AND context.list() returns [{id, kind:\"brand\", name}] (filtered by kind===\"brand\", lowercased) AND context.use(id) returns the ctx. Context shape: { name:\"Acme\", data:{ products:[\"Serum\",\"Cleanser\"], voice:\"calm premium\", positioning:\"...\", audience:\"...\", palette:[\"#hex\",...] } } — palette is FLAT hex strings; products falls back to data.range. WITHOUT a brand, NO model call fires at all (the app just waits for manual product entry; the shoot itself is agentic image-gen, not a completion). (3) STAGE 1 USES relay.complete (NOT stream): called as relay.complete({prompt, model:\"sonnet\", maxTokens:800}) and must resolve an object { text: \"<JSON array string>\" }. The text must contain a JSON array (```json fences tolerated/stripped) of ≥3 (ideally 6) objects; parser reads o.product, o.scene, o.direction, o.aspect and KEEPS only elements with non-empty product AND scene, so both must be present. aspect must be one of \"1:1\"|\"4:5\"|\"9:16\"|\"16:9\" (else coerced to \"1:1\"). This is unlike the streaming take.js template — do not stream stage 1. (4) storage.get must be callable and returning null is fine (forces fresh generation; syncFromRelayStorage awaits get on studio:sheet, studio:product, studio:setup, and generateShotList awaits get on studio:shotlist:<brand name> — returning null avoids serving a cached list). (5) STAGE 2 (the actual shoot, only after a user/programmatic click on a .look-shoot button or #shoot) is agentic: relay.stream({prompt, agentic:true, attachments?}). It is an async iterator of deltas {type:\"tool_proposed\",call:{name}} / {type:\"tool_result\",call:{name},result:{ok:true,content:[{text}]}} / {type:\"text\",text} / {type:\"done\",result:{text}} / {type:\"error\",error:{message}}. To yield a frame, emit a tool_result whose call.name INCLUDES \"generate_image\" (NOT media_upload/put_blob/media_confirm — those ref-step names are explicitly skipped so the user's own uploaded reference URL can't pass as the frame) with result.content[0].text containing an image URL ending .png/.jpg/.jpeg/.webp, OR emit a {type:\"text\"} / {type:\"done\",result:{text}} delta containing such a URL (extractUrl also matches \"rawUrl\"/\"url\"/\"minUrl\" JSON keys). Success appends a .shot card to #sheet and sets #shoot-line to \"frame developed ✓\".

result.harnessNotes:
Self-contained wrapp — imports ONLY @relay/sdk (whenRelayReady, mountConnect); no kit/ files, no other stages. scope.tools is [] and it NEVER sets agentic:true, so NO tool_result / image / Higgsfield path is exercised — genImage() is dead code here. Mock is window.claude at globalThis[PROVIDER_GLOBAL] with {isRelay:true, version, request({method,params}), on(evt,fn), removeListener}. Must handle: claude_capabilities (return {user:{name}}), claude_connect + claude_permissions (return a non-null grant {origin,models,tools,...} so onReady runs headless), claude_health (return {reachable:true,paired:true} or null), claude_storage (op get→{value:null} first run, set→ok), claude_context (op active→{context:{name,data:{products,positioning,palette,voice}}} — THIS is what triggers autostart), and streaming.

AUTOSTART GATE: onReady→autostart() fires start("",{fromContext:true}) ONLY if state.run is null AND context.active() returned a truthy brand. So to drive headless with ZERO input, the mock MUST return a brand from claude_context op:"active". If active() returns null, nothing fires and the user must type a one-line idea and click "Make the game" (or Enter) — start(input,{}) then runs the identical two stages.

STREAMING SHAPE (relay.stream): claude_stream returns {streamId}; then emit 'delta' events each carrying {streamId, ...delta}. Deltas the app reads: {type:"text",text} (accumulated), {type:"done"} or {type:"error",error:{message}} to end. Stage output is delivered as text deltas (one big chunk or many — the app concatenates). No tool_proposed/tool_result needed.

ROUTE BY PROMPT: Stage-1 prompt (no system) contains "attract-mode designer" / "invent 3 wildly different game concepts" / "1-3 word arcade title" → respond with a text delta whose body contains ONLY a JSON array of exactly 3 objects: [{"label","genre" (one of: endless runner, flappy, platformer, brick-breaker, dodge, snake-like, shooter, puzzle),"text","twist","recommended":true for exactly one}]. parseJsonArray slices the first "[" to last "]", so fences/prose are tolerated but a valid array is required (else "no pitches came back"). Stage-2 call passes system=GAME_SYSTEM ("expert HTML5 game developer", "single html file", "canvas-based", "requestAnimationFrame", "Respond with ONLY the html document") and prompt starting "Make this game:" → respond with a text delta containing a COMPLETE HTML document. extractHtml() requires a "<!doctype html" or "<html" start and a "</html>" end (else it throws "the model didn't return a complete game"); titleOf() reads "<title>...</title>" (≤60 chars) for the cabinet label, so include a <title>. The generated HTML is injected via iframe srcdoc with sandbox="allow-scripts" (opaque origin) — it must not reference window.claude/parent/fetch, but the harness doesn't enforce that; any self-contained <canvas> game HTML makes the success signal render. Steering chips / regenerate re-run stage-2 on the same pick; picking another card re-runs stage-2 for that pitch.

The mock must supply window.claude so the SDK yields a relay exposing: permissions() (return a truthy grant to take the returning-user boot path at line 73 → onReady → autostart), context.active() (return null for the plain typed-input path, OR a {name, data:{positioning|voice|audience|vibe,...}} persona/brand object to trigger zero-input autostart), storage.get/set (get may return null; loadState tolerates it), and on()/off(). AUTOSTART vs INPUT: stage-1 fires headless with ZERO typing ONLY when context.active() returns a persona/brand whose personaSeed(b) = `${name} — ${positioning||voice||audience||vibe}` is non-empty (autostart() at line 263, guarded by `if (state.run) return` so it never re-fires over a saved run). With no lent context, the app boots to a connected input box (placeholder "one line — who should we yearbook?") and needs a typed subject line + Enter/"Shoot" click to call start(). No file input, no camera, no getUserMedia anywhere — the subject is pure text, so this viral app is drivable headless without any photo. STAGE 1 (text): fires relay.stream({prompt}) with NO agentic flag. streamText() concatenates all {type:"text", text} deltas until the iterator ends, then parseJsonArray slices between the first '[' and last ']' (code fences ```json stripped, tolerated). Return a JSON array (2-4 elems; sliced to 4). Each element read for keys: label (string), vibe (string; falls back to o.text), imagePrompt (string; REQUIRED — elements with empty imagePrompt are filtered out and an all-empty array throws "malformed"), recommended (bool; if none true, first is forced). Minimal canned body: yield one text delta = `[{"label":"Class of '77","vibe":"Disco senior, feathered hair","imagePrompt":"vintage 1977 yearbook headshot, feathered hair, wide collar, amber studio haze, faded film, shoulders-up, no text","recommended":true},{"label":"'88 New Wave","vibe":"Teal laser backdrop","imagePrompt":"1988 New Wave yearbook headshot, blazer, teal laser backdrop, no logos","recommended":false},{"label":"'95 Grunge","vibe":"Flannel, mall studio","imagePrompt":"1995 grunge yearbook headshot, flannel, maroon backdrop, soft grain","recommended":false}]` then end the iterator. On success proposeEras bumps r.gen and immediately calls developAll(token()). STAGE 2 (agentic image): for each era (recommended sorted first), developPortrait fires relay.stream({prompt, agentic:true}). It reads deltas: on {type:"tool_result"} it checks result?.ok===true, joins result.content[].text, and matches IMG_URL_RE against that joined text; else accumulates {type:"text"} deltas and matches the URL there as fallback; {type:"error"} throws error.message. IMG_URL_RE = /(https?:...\.(png|jpe?g|webp))|"(rawUrl|url|minUrl)":"([^"]+)"/i — so a URL is found EITHER as a bare .png/.jpg/.jpeg/.webp link OR embedded as a JSON "rawUrl"/"url"/"minUrl":"..." pair. Simplest canned agentic result: yield {type:"tool_result", result:{ok:true, content:[{text:"https://img.example/portrait-1.png"}]}} then end. (Or just yield a {type:"text", text:"https://img.example/portrait-1.png"} delta.) Note the aspect_ratio is "4:5" here (the unused shared genImage() helper uses "16:9" but yearbook never calls it). Token guard: bumping run.gen (via a steer/start-over) abandons in-flight portraits, so keep the mock's per-call stream short. VISIBLE RESULT without any working image backend: because stage-1 never waits on an image (line comment enforced), returning a valid stage-1 array alone paints the full era grid + "developing…" spinners — that grid IS the primary success signal. Stage-2 image URLs only upgrade the spinner frames to ; even an erroring/absent Higgsfield leaves a legible "retry"/"develop portrait" fallback in each frame, so the app reaches a visible result headless from stage-1 alone.

To drive headless with ZERO user input, return a non-null object {name:"Acme", data:{positioning:"…"}}. autostart() builds seed = brand.name + " — " + brand.data.positioning and calls start(seed)→proposeTreatments() with no click/typing. Implement relay.stream(params) as an async iterator/generator. • STAGE 1 (params.prompt contains "comic director"/"four-panel comic strip", params.agentic is falsy): yield one or more {type:"text", text:} deltas whose concatenation is a JSON array string, then return (end iteration). streamText() accumulates .text; parseJsonArray() slices from first '[' to last ']' and JSON.parse. Wrapping the array in ```fences or prose is tolerated (regex-sliced). Emitting {type:"error",error:{message}} throws. • STAGE 2 (only fires on an 'ink this panel'/'ink the whole strip' click; params.agentic===true, prompt contains "generate_image"/"aspect_ratio"): yield {type:"tool_result", result:{ok:true, content:[{text:"https://cdn.example/panel1.png"}]}} then return. genImage reads result.content[].text, matches IMG_URL_RE = /(https?:\/\/…\.(png|jpe?g|webp))|"(?:rawUrl|url|minUrl)":"([^"]+)"/i — so the text must contain either a bare .png/.jpg/.jpeg/.webp URL OR a JSON fragment like {"rawUrl":"https://…"}. Alternatively skip tool_result and yield {type:"text",text:"https://cdn.example/panel1.png"} — the fallback also regex-matches acc. On success p.url is set and the `.panel .frame` renders an plus a "redraw" button; the `.cast .avatar` swaps its initial for the first drawn panel's image. Panels ink one-at-a-time (busyDrawing serializes consents). tool_result with ok falsy (or missing url) → "no panel came back" error in the `.pfail` slot. STREAM_TIMEOUT_MS is 180000; resolve promptly. Because the pipeline auto-advances on the recommended concept, a `.researching` row with status "writing the pages…" then a `.book-title` + `.pages` grid of `.page` cards (placeholder `.ph` "waiting to draw"/"drawing…") appear immediately after — that whole cascade is the visible proof stage-1 fired.
result.harnessNotes:
No photo/camera anywhere — the sole input is a text box ("Make the book" / Enter) or the lent-context cold-open. NO `kit/` imports; storybook.js is self-contained on `@relay/sdk`.

AUTOSTART: onReady() → autostart(). It fires stage-1 with ZERO input ONLY if a single context is lent: `brand` (from `relay.context.active()`) truthy → builds seed `"A children's picture book starring ${brand.name} — ${d.positioning||d.voice||d.audience}, as the hero…"` → start(seed). With no context and no saved run, autostart is a no-op and the app waits for a typed line. A bare permissions() grant alone does NOT boot it — hence bootsHeadless=false. Two ways to drive headless: (a) make `relay.context.active()` resolve a `{name, data:{positioning|voice|audience,...}}` object → zero-input cold-open; (b) leave context null and inject a typed line by calling the exposed `start('Maya, scared of the dark, meets a friendly star')` or dispatching input+Enter on the `.bindrow input`.

MOCK window.claude / relay surface required:
- whenRelayReady(2000,{installUrl}) → resolve a relay with a `connect` key; relay.permissions() → resolve a truthy grant (takes returning-user path → onReady). Alternatively mountConnect's onConnect(r) can be invoked to inject the relay.
- relay.on('permissionsChanged',fn) → register no-op.
- relay.context.active() → null (typed path) or brand object (cold-open path).
- relay.storage.get('storybook-state') → null (fresh; keeps state.run null so the start box renders). relay.storage.set → resolve.
- relay.stream(params) MUST return an async iterator (for-await). Route by prompt text:
  • Stage-1 (params.prompt, no agentic) → yield `{type:'text',text: <JSON ARRAY string>}` deltas. Shape each element: `{"label":"...","text":"...","age":"Ages 3–5","recommended":true}` (exactly one recommended; parser slices between first '[' and last ']', tolerates ```fences). Only label/text/age/recommended are read; array sliced to 4.
  • Stage-2a (params.prompt containing "write this book"/"json object", no agentic) → yield `{type:'text',text: <JSON OBJECT string>}`: `{"title":"...","style":"...","hero":"...","pages":[{"text":"...","scene":"..."}]}`. pages sliced to 10; a page needs text OR scene. Parser slices between first '{' and last '}'.
  • Stage-2b (params.agentic===true, prompt has "use the higgsfield generate_image tool") — called once per page. Yield `{type:'tool_result',result:{ok:true,content:[{text:'https://cdn.example/img.png'}]}}` then optionally `{type:'text',text:'https://cdn.example/img.png'}`. URL is extracted by IMG_URL_RE = `/(https?:\/\/[^\s"')]+\.(?:png|jpe?g|webp))|"(?:rawUrl|url|minUrl)"\s*:\s*"([^"]+)"/i` — so either a bare .png/.jpg/.jpeg/.webp URL in content text, or a `"url"/"rawUrl"/"minUrl":"..."` JSON field, works. `d.error` on any stream → thrown as Error.
- relay.complete() is defined in the template but Storybook never calls it (only relay.stream via streamText/genImage).

FAILURE MODES the mock must avoid: empty array → throws "no book ideas came back"; object with no pages array → throws "the pages came back malformed"; genImage returning no URL → page.imgStatus='error' ("no image came back — Redraw usually lands it"), non-fatal (words still render). streamText has a 180s race timeout, so resolve/close the iterator promptly. illusRun token cancels a stale illustration pass if start()/writeBook() re-fires, so keep one book in flight.

Stage-1 alone is enough for a visible result even if you stub Higgsfield to error — the book concept cards + written pages render regardless of illustrations.

result.harnessNotes: NOT a selfie/photo app — input is a single text line about a pet; the image is text-to-image via Higgsfield. No file input, camera, or getUserMedia anywhere. BOOT: petrait.js boots two ways, both need a mock window.claude relay. (a) Returning-user probe: whenRelayReady(2000,{installUrl}) must resolve a relay object that has a `connect` property (the check is `"connect" in r`); then r.permissions() must resolve NON-null (a grant) → onReady() runs → syncContext, loadState, render, autostart. (b) mountConnect's onConnect fires the same onReady after a live connect. Provide storage.get(returns null → fresh state {run:null}), storage.set(no-op resolve), and r.on('permissionsChanged',cb) as a no-op. AUTOSTART is conditional. autostart() only calls start() if a context is lent: it checks `if (state.run) return; if (brand) start(seedFromBrand(brand))`. brand = await relay.context.active(). So to drive stage-1 with ZERO user input, make context.active() resolve a brand object `{ name:"Acme", data:{ positioning:"...", voice:"...", palette:[...] } }` — seedFromBrand builds "the Acme mascot as a regal pet — " and stage 1 fires automatically. If context.active() returns null, NO autostart — #view shows the `.start` box with a text input (placeholder "one line about your pet…") and a "Paint" button; the harness must set the input value and click Paint (or dispatch Enter) to call start(input). Either path lands in the same proposeConcepts stage. STAGE 1 shape (relay.stream called with params that have NO `agentic` flag, prompt joined by "\n\n" containing the court-painter text): yield async-iterator deltas of `{type:"text", text:"..."}`. streamText concatenates all text deltas; you may chunk the payload or send it whole, then simply END the iterator (a {type:"done"} delta is harmless but not required — the loop ends on iterator completion; a {type:"error",error:{message}} delta throws). The concatenated text must contain a JSON array (fences ```json allowed — they're stripped; parseJsonArray grabs from first '[' to last ']'). Each element read for keys: label, text, imagePrompt, recommended. Example: [{"label":"Renaissance Oil","text":"Biscuit in a velvet doublet, unimpressed.","imagePrompt":"an orange tabby cat wearing a renaissance velvet doublet, oil painting, chiaroscuro lighting, palace backdrop, no text","recommended":true},{"label":"Astronaut","text":"...","imagePrompt":"...","recommended":false},{"label":"Boardroom CEO","text":"...","imagePrompt":"...","recommended":false}]. Only the first 4 are kept; if none has recommended:true the first is forced. Missing imagePrompt falls back to text. STAGE 2 shape (relay.stream called with `{prompt, agentic:true}`, prompt contains "generate_image tool to paint this portrait" and aspect_ratio "2:3") — auto-fired once for the recommended concept, and again when the user clicks/selects an unpainted card or the paint button. The wrapp reads the image URL two ways: (1) a delta `{type:"tool_result", result:{ok:true, content:[{text:"...URL..."}]}}` — it joins content[].text and matches IMG_URL_RE against it; (2) fallback: any `{type:"text", text:"...URL..."}` deltas are accumulated and matched after the loop. IMG_URL_RE = /(https?:\/\/[^\s"')]+\.(?:png|jpe?g|webp))|"(?:rawUrl|url|minUrl)"\s*:\s*"([^"]+)"/i — so the URL must EITHER end in .png/.jpg/.jpeg/.webp OR appear as a JSON key "url"/"rawUrl"/"minUrl":"...". Simplest mock: emit `{type:"tool_result", result:{ok:true, content:[{text:"{\"url\":\"https://cdn.example/portrait.png\"}"}]}}` (or just a bare `{type:"text", text:"https://cdn.example/portrait.png"}`), then end. On success c.imageUrl is set and an `` renders (note: an img onerror handler clears imageUrl and shows "the portrait link expired", so use a data: URI or a reachable image if you want the picture to actually paint rather than error). A tool_result with ok:false, or no URL match, leaves an .err "no portrait came back — retry usually lands on the second pass" with a "try again" button. The unused template helper genImage() (aspect "16:9", "generate an image of") is dead code in petrait — the live stage-2 path is paintPortrait's inline instruction (aspect "2:3", "paint this portrait"). Route stage-2 on the paintPortrait keywords.

result.harnessNotes:
Plumbing is byte-identical to take.js / redline.js; only the APP LOGIC below line 198 differs. No window.claude image/video/agentic paths are exercised — genImage() and relay.complete() are dead code here (scope.tools is []). The mock only needs relay.stream() plus the connect surface.

CONNECT/BOOT: the SDK's whenRelayReady(2000) must resolve to an object r where ("connect" in r) is true and r.permissions() resolves to a truthy grant; that path calls wire(r)+onReady() → syncContext()→loadState()→render()→autostart(). Equivalently mountConnect's onConnect(r) fires the same. Provide: r.permissions()->truthy; r.on(evt,cb) no-op; r.storage.get(key)->null (first run) / r.storage.set()->ok; r.context.active()->see below; r.stream(params)->async iterator (below).

AUTOSTART IS CONTEXT-GATED (the key nuance): autostart() no-ops if state.run exists, else fires start(seed) ONLY if `brand` is non-null, where brand = await relay.context.active(). So to drive fully headless with ZERO user input, the mock's context.active() MUST return {name:"<Brand>", data:{positioning:"<line>", ...}} (data is JSON.stringify'd, capped 2000 chars; data.positioning is optionally appended to the seed). That seeds input = "an anthem for <name> — <positioning>" and auto-runs stage-1→stage-2. If context.active() returns null, NOTHING auto-runs — render() shows the `.start` box; the harness must then type into `.start .bindrow input` and click `button.primary` ("Write it ▸") or press Enter to invoke start(). There is no global/exposed start() function; drive it via the DOM or via a lent context.

STREAM CONTRACT (both stages go through relay.stream(params), an async iterator; relay.complete is unused): yield deltas shaped {type:"text", text} (multiple deltas fine and preferred — stage-2 pipes each into #song-live via mdLite). Terminating the iterator ends the turn; optionally emit {type:"done"}. Emit {type:"error", error:{message}} to trip the error path. tool_proposed/tool_result deltas are ignored by streamText (no tools used).

ROUTING BY PROMPT (params.prompt is a single \n\n-joined string): 
 • STAGE 1 prompt contains "Propose 3 genuinely DISTINCT song concepts" + "Return ONLY a JSON array". Respond with text that is a JSON array (fences/prose tolerated — parseJsonArray slices first '[' to last ']'). Each element read for keys label, hook, mood, recommended, e.g. [{"label":"Stadium synth-pop","hook":"we don't back down","mood":"defiant, euphoric","recommended":true},{"label":"Acoustic ballad","hook":"...","mood":"tender","recommended":false},{"label":"Trap anthem","hook":"...","mood":"gritty","recommended":false}]. Must be a non-empty array or start() throws "no concepts came back". Exactly one recommended (code force-sets [0] if none). On success it maps to concepts and AUTO-ADVANCES into stage-2 with the recommended concept.
 • STAGE 2 prompt contains "Write a complete, ORIGINAL song about" + "Suno-style" + "Output EXACTLY this markdown" (params.maxTokens:2000). Respond with plain markdown text (no JSON) — a "## Title", **[Verse 1]** / **[Chorus]** / **[Bridge]** sections, then "---", then a **Generation prompt** paragraph. The full accumulated text is stored as r.song and rendered; no keys are parsed.

STEERING/RESELECT (optional, post-boot): clicking a different `.opt` card calls writeSong(id) (re-runs stage-2 only); the steer chips ["more anthemic","make it a ballad","punch up the hook","add a bridge"] or the steer text box append to r.steers and re-run stage-2. All still hit the STAGE 2 route. State persists per-origin via storage under key "anthem-state"; a saved run suppresses autostart on reconnect.

It then auto-advances (ONE-GO) to stage-2, which streams markdown into div.md#reading-live under a "the reading" header. Earliest concrete stage-1 signal = the three .opt cards with one .rec badge.
result.harnessNotes: NO photo/camera — the single input is a typed dream (textarea.dream-input → "Interpret ▸" button, or ⌘/Ctrl+Enter). autostart() only fires the pipeline with ZERO input if a context is lent (relay.context.active() returns non-null → seeds start(name+" — "+data.positioning, {seeded:true})). To drive headless two ways: (A) typed path — mock relay.context.active()→null, then call start() by dispatching input+click, or (B) cold-open — mock relay.context.active() to resolve {name:"Acme", data:{positioning:"..."}} so onReady→autostart fires stage-1 with no typed line. Boot: (async IIFE) awaits whenRelayReady(2000,{installUrl}); the returned object must have a "connect" property and relay.permissions() must resolve TRUTHY → triggers onReady() → syncContext()+loadState()+render()+autostart(). Provide relay.storage.get(key)→null (or JSON string) and relay.storage.set→ok, relay.context.active(), relay.on(evt,cb) no-op, relay.permissions()→{}. The ONLY LLM primitive actually used is relay.stream(params) (an async iterator); relay.complete is never called. askJson/askJsonArray wrap streamText which reads deltas of shape {type:"text",text}, {type:"tool_proposed",call:{name}}, {type:"error",error:{message}}. Route the mock stream by prompt keywords: - Stage-1 (lens keywords): yield one {type:"text", text:'[{"text":"...","recommended":false},{"text":"...","recommended":true},{"text":"...","recommended":false}]'} then finish. parseJsonArray slices from first "[" to last "]"; array MUST be exactly 3 elements IN ORDER (mapped by index to jungian/emotional/omens); each element read for .text and .recommended (exactly one true, else code forces index 0). - Stage-2 (interpret keywords, maxTokens 1200): yield several {type:"text",text:"..."} markdown chunks; rendered live into #reading-live via mdLite. No JSON needed. - Stage-3 image (agentic:true, "generate_image"/"surreal"): yield {type:"tool_result", result:{ok:true, content:[{text:"https://img.example/dream.png"}]}} then end. genImage matches IMG_URL_RE = /(https?:...\.(png|jpe?g|webp))|"(rawUrl|url|minUrl)":"([^"]+)"/i against joined content[].text; falls back to matching accumulated {type:"text"}. Returns URL → r.image.url → . Stage-3 is button-gated (.primary.conjure "Conjure the dreamscape ✦"), never autostarts, and is skippable — stages 1+2 alone give a visible result.
